title: lindsaea prolongata
intro: Lindsaea prolongata, also known as the Long-leaf Lindsaea or Australian Fishbone Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the genus Lindsaea, a group of epiphytic ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Australia and the Pacific Islands, and is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lindsaea prolongata include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Traditionally, the plant is prepared by infusing its leaves in hot water to make a tea or by combining it with other herbs in a tincture or poultice.
